commonIssues:
Difficulty in identifying the correct Sub-Registrar and booking slots.

How to fix:

  • Use the DORIS Portal to find the jurisdictional SRO based on your address and book an appointment online.

While a registered Will is strong, third parties (like banks or DDA) often require probate.

How to fix:

  • Inform beneficiaries that they may need to file a probate petition in the Delhi District Court after the testator's demise to enforce the Will.

Using vague language or failing to update the Will after major life events.

How to fix:

  • List all assets with absolute clarity. Register a new Will or a Codicil if assets are sold or new heirs are born.

Beneficiaries often face lawsuits alleging the testator was pressured or unfit.

How to fix:

  • Attach a medical certificate of fitness and have a doctor as one of the attesting witnesses if possible.

A later unregistered Will can render a previous registered Will invalid.

How to fix:

  • Always register subsequent Wills and explicitly state that all previous Wills and Codicils are revoked.
